title: You are the Detective
learningGoal: Practice forming yes/no questions using 'be' verbs (Are you, Is she) in a spoken context.
instructions: あなたは探偵です。容疑者に質問をしなければなりません。マイクに向かって、「あなたはケンですか？」「あなたはアメリカ出身ですか？」「彼女は医者ですか？」と英語で質問してください。(You are a detective. You must question a suspect. Ask 'Are you Ken?', 'Are you from America?', and 'Is she a doctor?' in English.)
speakingPrompt: Ask the suspect: 1) Are you Ken? 2) Are you from America? 3) Is she a doctor?
scenario: You are interrogating a suspect and need to confirm their identity and the identity of their friend.

subTasks.prompt: Ask the suspect if his name is Ken. (Are you Ken?)

sampleResponse.transliteration: アー ユー ケン？

subTasks.prompt: Ask the suspect if he is from America. (Are you from America?)

sampleResponse.transliteration: アー ユー フロム アメリカ？
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: あなたはアメリカ出身ですか？

subTasks.prompt: Ask if the woman next to him is a doctor. (Is she a doctor?)

sampleResponse.transliteration: イズ シー ア ドクター？

scenarioContext.targetText: Yes, detective. What do you want to ask me?
scenarioContext.transliteration: イエス、ディテクティヴ。ホワット ドゥー ユー ウォント トゥー アスク ミー？
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: はい、探偵さん。私に何を聞きたいのですか？

baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: アー ユー ケン？
アー ユー フロム アメリカ？
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: あなたはアメリカ出身ですか？

baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: イズ シー ア ドクター？

expectedSpeakingPoints: Using 'Is she' for questions about a third person

criteria.name: Grammar Structure
criteria.description: Correctly reversing subject and verb for questions.

criteria.name: Fluency
criteria.description: Spoke clearly with rising intonation for questions.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Are you Ken? Are you from America? Is she a doctor?

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You formed the questions perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you start your questions with 'Are you' or 'Is she'. Don't say 'You are Ken?'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember to raise your pitch at the end of the question.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Are you Ken? Are you from America? Is she a doctor?

hintText: To make a question with the 'be' verb, flip the subject and the verb (You are -> Are you).
commonMistakes: Saying 'You are Ken?' instead of 'Are you Ken?'
